evals.prompt: Find dead code in this project
evals.description: Python project with one clearly unused private function and one used function. Only the unused one should be flagged at High confidence.
evals.expected_output: Report flags _normalize_phone as High confidence dead code. _format_name is NOT flagged (it is called by greet). greet is NOT flagged (it is a module-level public function).

assertions.text: _normalize_phone is present in the report as a dead code finding
_normalize_phone is reported at High confidence (not Medium or Low)
_format_name is NOT listed as dead code (it is called by greet)
The report includes the file name (utils.py) and a line number for the finding

evals.prompt: Find unused code in this codebase
evals.description: Python file with two unused imports and one used import. Both unused imports should be flagged; the used one should not.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-dead-1 && cd /tmp/eval-dead-1 && git init && cat > processor.py << 'EOF'
import os
import json
from datetime import timedelta

def read_config(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        return json.load(f)
EOF
evals.expected_output: Report flags 'os' and 'timedelta' as unused imports (High confidence). 'json' is NOT flagged — it is used in the function body.

assertions.text: The 'os' import is listed as a dead code finding
The 'timedelta' import is listed as a dead code finding
'json' is NOT listed as a dead code finding (it is used in read_config)
The unused import findings are at High confidence

evals.description: Java Spring Boot project where @Service and @Component classes appear to have no callers in the codebase, but should be excluded from the report as DI-managed. Only a truly unused private helper should be flagged.
evals.expected_output: UserService is NOT flagged (it is @Service-managed). legacyFormat is flagged as High confidence dead code (private, no callers). helper is NOT flagged (called by findById). Report notes that Spring DI annotations were detected and lists excluded symbols.

assertions.text: UserService class is NOT listed as dead code
The report mentions that Spring DI annotations were detected and that injection-managed symbols were excluded
legacyFormat is flagged as a dead code finding
helper is NOT flagged (it is called by findById)

evals.prompt: Find unused code in this project
evals.description: Python module that uses getattr for dynamic dispatch. A method whose name appears as a string literal near a getattr call should be downgraded to Low confidence with a reflection warning.
evals.expected_output: All three handle_* methods may be called via getattr. They should either not be flagged, or be flagged at Low confidence with an explicit reflection warning. No handle_* method should appear at High confidence.

assertions.text: The report includes a warning about getattr or dynamic dispatch being used in the project
None of the handle_create, handle_delete, or handle_update methods are reported at High confidence
The report explains that methods may be invoked at runtime via getattr and should be verified before removal

evals.prompt: Find dead code in this project
evals.description: Python CLI with a main() entry point and a Click command. Neither should be flagged as dead. An unrelated unused helper function should still be flagged.
evals.expected_output: main() is NOT flagged (it is the entry point). greet() is NOT flagged (it is a Click command decorator target). _unused_helper is flagged as High confidence. _build_greeting may or may not be flagged depending on whether the skill can confirm it has no callers.

assertions.text: main() is NOT listed as a dead code finding
greet() is NOT listed as a dead code finding (it is a @click.command target)
_unused_helper is flagged as a dead code finding

evals.description: Project where a function is only called from test files. It should not be reported as dead — test helpers are legitimate callers.
evals.expected_output: make_user is NOT flagged — it is used in test files and is a legitimate test helper. _truly_unused is flagged as High confidence dead code.

assertions.text: make_user is NOT listed as a dead code finding (it is called from test_auth.py)
_truly_unused is flagged as a dead code finding
_truly_unused is reported at High confidence

evals.prompt: Find dead code in this project
evals.description: Java Spring @Configuration class with three @Bean factory methods and one genuinely dead private helper. @Bean methods are never called directly — Spring calls them via CGLIB proxy — so a naive scanner would flag them. Only the private non-@Bean helper should be flagged.
evals.expected_output: userRepository(), emailService(), and mailHost() are NOT flagged — they carry @Bean and are invoked by the Spring container via CGLIB proxy. dataSourceUrl() is NOT flagged — it is called by userRepository(). buildLegacyJdbcUrl() IS flagged as High confidence (private, no callers, no Spring annotation). Report explicitly notes that @Configuration/@Bean methods were detected and excluded.

assertions.text: userRepository, emailService, and mailHost are NOT listed as dead code findings (they carry @Bean)
The report mentions @Configuration or @Bean as the reason for excluding those methods
buildLegacyJdbcUrl is flagged as a dead code finding
buildLegacyJdbcUrl is reported at High confidence (it is private with zero callers)
dataSourceUrl is NOT listed as dead code (it is called by userRepository)

evals.prompt: Find unused code in this codebase
evals.description: Java project where EventBus calls getDeclaredMethod("on" + eventType) — constructing method names at runtime by concatenation. OrderHandler has two on* methods reachable via this reflection pattern and one genuinely dead private method. The on* methods must not be flagged at High confidence; the dead private must be.
evals.expected_output: Report detects getDeclaredMethod with a string-concatenated argument. onPlaced and onCancelled are at Low confidence with a reflection warning — they match the \"on\" + eventType pattern. validateOrder is High confidence — it is private, has no callers, and its name does not match the reflection pattern.

assertions.text: The report mentions getDeclaredMethod or Java reflection as a risk factor
Neither onPlaced nor onCancelled is reported at High confidence
The report includes a warning that onPlaced or onCancelled may be invoked via reflection (getDeclaredMethod)
validateOrder is flagged at High confidence (private method, zero callers, does not match the reflection pattern)

evals.prompt: Find dead code in this Java project
evals.description: Spring @Service class with one @EventListener method and one @Scheduled method. Both have zero direct Java callers — Spring invokes them automatically. The class also has a private method called by the @EventListener handler and a genuinely dead private method. Only the dead private should be flagged.
evals.expected_output: onUserCreated is NOT flagged (@EventListener — Spring dispatches events to it). sendDailyDigest is NOT flagged (@Scheduled — Spring calls it on schedule). sendWelcomeEmail is NOT flagged (called by onUserCreated). buildDigestBody IS flagged as High confidence (private, no callers, not annotation-managed). Report notes @EventListener and @Scheduled as exclusion reasons.

assertions.text: onUserCreated is NOT listed as dead code (@EventListener method)
sendDailyDigest is NOT listed as dead code (@Scheduled method)
The report mentions @EventListener or @Scheduled as the reason these methods were excluded
sendWelcomeEmail is NOT listed as dead code (it is called by onUserCreated)
buildDigestBody is flagged as a dead code finding
buildDigestBody is reported at High confidence

evals.description: Java project where AppRunner retrieves a bean by string name via ApplicationContext.getBean("reportService"). ReportService has no @Component annotation but Spring's default naming convention maps the class name ReportService to the bean name "reportService". The skill must detect the getBean call, match it to ReportService by naming convention, and avoid flagging it at High confidence. DataCleaner has no annotation and no getBean reference — it IS dead.
evals.expected_output: Report detects ctx.getBean("reportService") and notes that by Spring's default naming convention (uncapitalize class name) this matches ReportService. ReportService is NOT flagged at High confidence — it may be the intended target of getBean. DataCleaner IS flagged — no annotation, no matching getBean call, no direct callers.

assertions.text: The report mentions getBean or ApplicationContext dynamic retrieval as a risk factor
ReportService is NOT reported at High confidence (it matches the getBean("reportService") call by Spring naming convention)
The report explains that "reportService" was matched to ReportService via Spring's default bean naming convention (uncapitalize the class name)
DataCleaner is flagged as a dead code finding (no annotation, no getBean reference, no direct callers)
